world.title: Trade in transition: financing, competitiveness and the future of SA agriculture
world.desc: South African agricultural exports have grown from under $2 billion in 2000 to more than $15 billion in 2025 – but in an era of rising protectionism and shifting global trade rules, maintaining this trajectory requires more than production capacity or market access. Writing for African Farming, Ovizikhungo Sicwetsha, Head of Transactional Banking for the mid-corporate segment at Nedbank, argues that the ability of financial institutions to structure, fund and de-risk growth in a fragmented world will be decisive – and that Africa’s expanding markets offer South Africa’s agricultural sector its greatest long-term opportunity.
